text:
Department of Neurology 

Clinic Date: 28 September 2024 

Dr Amelia Carter  
St. George’s University Hospitals NHS Foundation Trust  
Department of Neurology, Blackshaw Road, London SW17 0QT  

Dear Dr Amelia Carter,  

Emily Turner, DOB: 14-03-1992, Hospital No: H294771 NHS No. 9081763452  
Flat 12, 25 Jubilee Gardens, London SE1 7PB 

Diagnoses: 

Recurrent unprovoked seizures of uncertain classification 

Current Medication: 

Levetiracetam 1000 mg bd  
Clobazam 10 mg nocte (intermittent, rescue for seizure clusters) 

Plan: 

- Continue Levetiracetam 1000 mg twice daily  
- Maintain seizure diary and record potential precipitants  
- Optimise sleep hygiene; avoid alcohol excess and missed meals  
- Safety counselling provided regarding bathing, heights, and use of machinery  
- DVLA guidance discussed: must not drive and should notify DVLA as per current standards  
- Arrange MRI brain with epilepsy protocol and routine plus sleep-deprived EEG  
- Bloods today: FBC, U&E, LFTs, Vitamin D  
- Follow up in 8–10 weeks to review investigations and frequency  

I reviewed Emily Turner in clinic today following multiple recent events. She has had a seizure so far this month, five in Aug, one in Jul and 5 in Jun, with events reported from both daytime and nocturnal periods. She describes an abrupt loss of awareness in some episodes with witnessed generalised shaking and tongue biting on two occasions; on other days, she reports brief staring and loss of conversation with rapid recovery. No clear focal onset has been identified from her or witness accounts. There has been no recent intercurrent illness. She notes poor sleep related to shift work and increased stress over the summer. There is no known family history of seizures. 

Neurological examination today was normal. Cardiovascular observations were stable. There are no focal neurological deficits. 

We discussed potential triggers and agreed practical measures to reduce sleep deprivation and keep a consistent routine. She will continue Levetiracetam at the current dose and use Clobazam short-term if she experiences a clustering pattern (not more than 10 days per month). She will keep a detailed seizure and trigger diary, including timing, context, and any aura, to assist classification. Given the ongoing events, I have requested MRI brain with epilepsy protocol and both routine and sleep-deprived EEG to further evaluate. 

I have advised her not to drive and to inform the DVLA, in line with current regulations. We also reviewed general safety measures including supervised bathing/showers, avoiding heights and open flames when alone, and discussing seizure first aid with household members. Written information was provided. 

I will review her in 8–10 weeks after investigations. Should the frequency increase or she experiences new prolonged events, she will contact the clinic sooner via the epilepsy nurse line. 

With best wishes,

entities.text: She has had a seizure so far this month, five in Aug, one in Jul and 5 in Jun
She has had a seizure so far this month, five in Aug, one in Jul and 5 in Jun, with events reported from both daytime and nocturnal periods

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: Identify explicit seizure counts by month mentioned in the letter: Jun: 5, Jul: 1, Aug: 5, Sep (so far this month): 1. These are within the last four months (June to September). Sum to get total seizures over that period: 5 + 1 + 5 + 1 = 12 in 4 months. Choose the highest applicable frequency; the letter provides discrete monthly counts rather than a more frequent rate, so normalize as total per 4 months.
seizure_frequency_number: She has had a seizure so far this month, five in Aug, one in Jul and 5 in Jun
